description: FutSpring Stats & Rankings — global user stats, per-pelada ranking, stats computation on finalization, and profile page stats display

userStories.title: Create Stats and Ranking entities and extend finalize to compute them
userStories.description: As the system, I need global Stats and per-pelada Ranking entities created and populated during daily finalization so that aggregate performance data is persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Stats JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), user (OneToOne User, required), goals (int, default 0), assists (int, default 0), matchesPlayed (int, default 0), wins (int, default 0), puskasDates (List<LocalDate>, stored as @ElementCollection in a stats_puskas_dates join table)

Create Ranking JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), pelada (ManyToOne Pelada, required), user (ManyToOne User, required), goals (int, default 0), assists (int, default 0), matchesPlayed (int, default 0), wins (int, default 0); unique constraint on (pelada, user)
Create StatsRepository extending JpaRepository<Stats, Long> with: findByUser(User user) returning Optional<Stats>
Create RankingRepository extending JpaRepository<Ranking, Long> with: findByPelada(Pelada pelada) and findByPeladaAndUser(Pelada pelada, User user) returning Optional<Ranking>

Extend DailyService.finalizeDaily() within the same @Transactional block: (1) determine the winning team (position 1 from LeagueTable, or null if tied); (2) for each confirmed player: upsert Ranking entry for the pelada (create if not exists, increment goals/assists/matchesPlayed/wins); upsert global Stats record (create if not exists, increment goals/assists/matchesPlayed/wins); if this player is the puskasWinner, append today's LocalDate to stats.puskasDates; (3) all upserts use findByX().orElse(new entity) then save
Finalization idempotency guard: check daily.isFinished at start of finalizeDaily; throw AppException(HttpStatus.CONFLICT, 'Daily already finalized') if true
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend ranking endpoint and frontend ranking table on Pelada page
userStories.description: As a member, I want to see how players rank in our pelada so I know who the top performers are.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/peladas/:id/ranking — members only (403 for non-members); returns List<RankingDTO> sorted by goals desc; players with no Ranking entry still appear with zeros (build from pelada.getMembers() and merge with existing Ranking rows)
RankingDTO fields: userId, username, userImage, goals, assists, matchesPlayed, wins
Add getRanking(peladaId, userEmail) method to a RankingService (or DailyService if simpler) annotated @Transactional(readOnly=true)
Add GET /peladas/{id}/ranking endpoint to PeladaController (or new RankingController)
Add RankingDTO interface to frontend src/types/daily.ts (or src/types/ranking.ts)
Add getRanking(peladaId) API function to src/api/peladas.ts (or src/api/ranking.ts) using existing Axios client

Add a Ranking tab or section to PeladaDetailPage: fetches getRanking(peladaId); shows shadcn/ui Table with columns: Player, Goals, Assists, Matches, Wins; default sort goals desc; column headers are clickable to sort asc/desc client-side; skeleton 3-row loading; empty state if no data
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Daily stats and awards display on finished Daily page
userStories.description: Player stats table and award cards on the finished daily page are already implemented in the daily cycle (US-019). Marking complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Already implemented: finished Daily page shows player stats table (Player, Goals, Assists, Matches, Wins sorted by goals desc)
Already implemented: finished Daily page shows award cards for Puskas and Wiltball winners with avatar and username

userStories.title: Backend user stats endpoint and frontend Profile page with global stats
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my overall performance stats across all peladas on my profile.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/users/:id/stats — any authenticated user can view any user's stats; returns StatsDTO
StatsDTO fields: userId, username, goals, assists, matchesPlayed, wins, puskasDates (List<LocalDate>)
If no Stats record exists for the user, return zeros and empty puskasDates list
Add getStats(userId, callerEmail) method to a StatsService (or UserService) annotated @Transactional(readOnly=true)
Add GET /users/{id}/stats endpoint to UserController (or new StatsController)
Add StatsDTO interface to frontend src/types/stats.ts (or src/types/user.ts)
Add getUserStats(userId) API function using existing Axios client

Create ProfilePage at /profile/:id (lazy-loaded): fetches user info and getUserStats(userId); shows stats summary cards (Goals, Assists, Matches, Wins); shows Recharts BarChart with goals and assists as two bars; skeleton loading; empty/zero state renders without error
Add /profile/:id route to App.tsx (replace any existing placeholder)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend Puskas award history on Profile page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see when I've won the Puskas award on my profile page.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page shows a 'Puskas Award History' section below the stats chart
Section lists each date in stats.puskasDates formatted as a readable date string (e.g. 'March 21, 2026')
Dates sorted most recent first
Empty state message 'No Puskas wins yet.' if puskasDates is empty
Section uses skeleton loading while stats are loading
Typecheck passes
